title: The Elves and the Shoemaker
content:
Once upon a time there was a kind shoemaker who had fallen on hard times. One evening he found he had only one piece of leather left, just enough for one pair of shoes. He cut it out carefully, laid it on his workbench, and went to bed.

When he came downstairs the next morning, he could not believe his eyes. There on the workbench sat a perfect, finished pair of shoes! The stitching was so fine and even that no craftsman in the whole town could have done better.

He sold the shoes that very morning for a good price and bought leather for two more pairs. He cut them out that evening, laid them on the bench, and went to bed. In the morning, two perfect pairs were waiting for him. This happened again and again!

The shoemaker and his wife grew curious. Who was making the shoes? They decided to stay up one night and watch. At the stroke of midnight, two tiny elves crept in and climbed onto the workbench. They worked with flying fingers, stitching and hammering, until every shoe was perfect.

The couple was filled with wonder and gratitude. They noticed the little elves had no clothes and must be cold on winter nights. Together they spent several days sewing tiny shirts, little trousers, and miniature shoes, all exactly the right size for two small elves. They left the gifts on the workbench and hid to watch.

At midnight the elves arrived, found the tiny clothes, and held them up with delighted cries. They dressed themselves quickly and danced and leaped about the bench with pure joy before skipping out the door and disappearing forever. But the shoemaker's fortune had already been made, and he and his wife lived happily and gratefully for the rest of their days.

author: Brothers Grimm
